description: TypoKit Performance Optimization Phase 2 — Close Bun gap to <5% vs raw Bun.serve(), reduce Node middleware overhead from 40-56% to <5%, reduce Node DB overhead from 7% to <5%, all while maintaining Phase 1 wins

userStories.title: Benchmark app — use JSON_HEADERS constant in all handlers
userStories.description: As a benchmark developer, I want all benchmark handler functions to use the shared JSON_HEADERS constant from @typokit/core so that benchmark results reflect real-world best practices and don't penalize TypoKit with avoidable per-request header object allocations.
acceptanceCriteria: All handler return values in packages/benchmarks/src/apps/shared-routes.ts use JSON_HEADERS from @typokit/core instead of inline { "content-type": "application/json" }
Same change applied to shared-routes-bun.ts and shared-routes-common.ts
Import JSON_HEADERS from @typokit/core at the top of each file
All benchmark scenarios continue to pass health checks and return correct responses
Run benchmarks build to verify: pnpm nx build benchmarks
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck benchmarks

userStories.title: Replace enrichedReq spread with params mutation
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want the request params to be set via direct property assignment instead of object spread so that the handler hot path avoids creating a new object per request. In packages/server-native/src/index.ts line 402, replace `const enrichedReq: TypoKitRequest = { ...req, params }` with `req.params = params` and use `req` directly downstream.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace `const enrichedReq: TypoKitRequest = { ...req, params }` with `req.params = params` (direct mutation) in packages/server-native/src/index.ts
Remove the enrichedReq variable — use req directly in all downstream code (middleware, handler call, serialization)
Verify that normalizeRequest() in both platform-node/src/index.ts and platform-bun/src/index.ts create fresh objects per request (safe to mutate) — add a code comment confirming this
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test server-native
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck server-native

userStories.title: Skip Object.assign for empty middleware returns
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want compileMiddlewareChain() to skip the Object.assign(ctx, added) call when middleware returns undefined, null, or {} so that no-op middleware has zero cost. In packages/core/src/middleware.ts, add a guard before every Object.assign call in the compiled chain.
acceptanceCriteria: In compileMiddlewareChain() in packages/core/src/middleware.ts, add a guard before Object.assign: skip if added is null, undefined, or has no own enumerable keys
Use a fast check: `if (added != null && typeof added === 'object')` then use a for...in early-exit check (faster than Object.keys().length) before calling Object.assign
Apply the same guard to the single-middleware case (the 1-entry branch around line 124)
Middleware that returns actual context values (e.g., { user: ... }) continues to work correctly — add a test case for this
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test core
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck core

userStories.title: Eliminate per-request mwReq reconstruction in middleware wrapper
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want the server-native middleware wrapper to stop creating a new TypoKitRequest object per middleware invocation. In packages/server-native/src/index.ts registerRoutes() (around lines 444-461), the middleware wrapper closure creates a fresh mwReq: TypoKitRequest for every middleware call by copying from currentReq + input. Instead, pass the request object directly.
acceptanceCriteria: The middleware wrapper in registerRoutes() in packages/server-native/src/index.ts reuses the request object directly instead of constructing a new mwReq per invocation
Pass the request as a closure variable to the compiled middleware chain instead of reconstructing from currentReq + input fields
Remove the currentReq module-level variable (it stores mutable state across requests which is a code smell)
The compiled middleware chain receives the request object reference, not a copy
All existing middleware tests pass: pnpm nx test core && pnpm nx test server-native
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck core && pnpm nx typecheck server-native
userStories.notes: Depends on US-026 (params mutation) being complete so req is already mutated in place

userStories.title: Eliminate per-request MiddlewareInput allocation
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want the middleware compiled chain to receive request fields as direct arguments instead of allocating a MiddlewareInput wrapper object per request. In packages/core/src/middleware.ts lines 179-185, the N-middleware compiled function creates { headers, body, query, params, ctx } per request. Change the internal signature to accept (req, ctx) directly.
acceptanceCriteria: The compiled middleware function signature changes internally to accept (req: TypoKitRequest, ctx: RequestContext) directly, extracting headers, body, query, params from req inline when calling middleware handlers
No MiddlewareInput object is allocated in the hot path of the compiled chain
The public MiddlewareInput type remains exported from @typokit/core for user-facing middleware definitions
The middleware handler wrapper in server-native registerRoutes() (lines 444-461) passes req directly instead of constructing input fields
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test core && pnpm nx test server-native
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck core && pnpm nx typecheck server-native
userStories.notes: Depends on US-024 (eliminate mwReq) — the middleware wrapper already passes req directly after that change

userStories.title: Optimize createRequestContext allocation
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want createRequestContext() to use lazy creation and prototype-based allocation so that context cost is minimized per request. In packages/server-native/src/index.ts line 405, every request calls createRequestContext() even for routes with no middleware that never read context.
acceptanceCriteria: createRequestContext() is only called when the handler or middleware actually needs context (lazy creation via getter or flag-based detection at registration time)
For routes with no middleware, context is created only if the handler signature requires it (detected at registration time by checking if the handler has 2+ parameters)
Context object uses Object.create(baseContext) with a shared prototype for common fields, only overriding per-request fields (requestId, startTime)
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test server-native
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck server-native

userStories.title: Reduce middleware overhead via synchronous no-op detection
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want the compiled middleware chain to detect when all middleware are synchronous no-ops at registration time and replace the chain with a synchronous identity function. The benchmark uses 5 no-op middleware layers returning {}. The compiled chain still awaits each sequentially, creating 5 microtask suspensions per request.
acceptanceCriteria: compileMiddlewareChain() in packages/core/src/middleware.ts accepts an optional noOp hint on MiddlewareEntry (e.g., entry.noOp: boolean) that marks middleware as no-op at registration time
When all entries are marked noOp, the compiled chain returns (req, ctx) => ctx synchronously — no Promise, no async
When a mix of noOp and real middleware exist, only the real middleware are included in the compiled chain (noOp entries are filtered out)
The server-native middleware wrapper in registerRoutes() sets the noOp hint when the benchmark's no-op middleware pattern is detected (handler returns empty object synchronously)
Middleware with actual side effects (logging, auth) that are NOT marked noOp continue to work correctly
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test core && pnpm nx test server-native
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck core && pnpm nx typecheck server-native
userStories.notes: Depends on US-025 (empty assign guard) and US-027 (MiddlewareInput elimination)

userStories.title: Bun — replace Proxy-based headers with direct access
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want Bun request headers accessed without a Proxy wrapper so that header reads don't incur trap overhead on every access. In packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts, normalizeHeaders() wraps the native Headers in new Proxy() (lines 76-141). Every headers[key] access triggers get trap -> native.has() -> native.get().
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the Proxy-based normalizeHeaders() in packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts with a plain object that copies headers eagerly on construction
Use headers.forEach((value, key) => obj[key] = value) which is the fastest iteration method for Web API Headers
Object.keys(normalizedHeaders) returns all header names (same ownKeys behavior as before)
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test platform-bun
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck platform-bun
userStories.notes: Starts the independent Bun optimization track

userStories.title: Bun — pre-allocate static Response objects for JSON
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want common JSON response patterns to use pre-allocated header objects so that buildResponse() in packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts avoids per-request new Headers() construction. Currently it iterates response.headers with for...in and calls respHeaders.set() per header, then constructs new Response(body, { status, headers: respHeaders }).
acceptanceCriteria: Create module-level constants in packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts: JSON_RESPONSE_INIT_200, JSON_RESPONSE_INIT_400, JSON_RESPONSE_INIT_404, JSON_RESPONSE_INIT_500 — each with { status, headers } using a pre-allocated Headers object

buildResponse() detects when response headers match the JSON content-type pattern (only key is content-type with value application/json) and uses the pre-allocated init object for the matching status code
For non-matching headers, fall back to current per-request construction
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test platform-bun
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck platform-bun

userStories.title: Bun — sync normalizeRequest for GET requests
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want normalizeRequest() to be synchronous for GET/HEAD/DELETE requests so that the Bun hot path avoids async overhead. Currently normalizeRequest() is async because it awaits req.json()/req.text() for POST bodies, but GET/HEAD/DELETE have no body so the await is unnecessary.
acceptanceCriteria: Split normalizeRequest() in packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts into two functions: normalizeRequestSync(req: Request): TypoKitRequest for bodyless methods (GET, HEAD, DELETE, OPTIONS) and normalizeRequestAsync(req: Request): Promise<TypoKitRequest> for methods with bodies (POST, PUT, PATCH)
createBunServer() calls the sync variant when req.method is GET, HEAD, DELETE, or OPTIONS
The sync path creates zero Promises and has no await expressions
The async path handles POST/PUT/PATCH body parsing exactly as before
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test platform-bun
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck platform-bun
userStories.notes: Depends on US-021 (Proxy headers removed) since normalizeRequest calls normalizeHeaders

userStories.title: Bun — inline Response construction
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want the Bun response path to construct Response objects with minimal allocations. buildResponse() in packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts (lines 206-237) creates new Headers, iterates response.headers, JSON.stringifys body, then constructs new Response. Bun's Response constructor accepts plain objects for headers — use this instead of new Headers().
acceptanceCriteria: buildResponse() detects JSON object bodies and calls JSON.stringify() + new Response() in a single expression without intermediate variables for the headers
For JSON responses, use a pre-allocated plain object { 'content-type': 'application/json' } as headers (not new Headers()) since Bun's Response constructor accepts plain objects
Remove the for...in loop that builds a new Headers object per request for the JSON fast path
Non-JSON responses (strings, Buffers, null) continue to work correctly via the existing path
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test platform-bun
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck platform-bun
userStories.notes: Depends on US-023 (static Response objects) — builds on the pre-allocated init constants

userStories.title: Bun fast-path — bypass normalizeRequest/buildResponse for GET JSON
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want the Bun server path in server-native to skip the full normalize→handle→buildResponse round-trip for simple GET endpoints so that Bun throughput approaches raw Bun.serve(). Currently every request converts Request→TypoKitRequest→TypoKitResponse→Response. For GET routes with no middleware/validators, handle the raw Request directly and return a Response inline.
acceptanceCriteria: createBunServer() in packages/platform-bun/src/index.ts accepts an optional fast handler callback: (req: Request) => Response | null that receives the raw Request and returns a Response directly, or null to fall back to the normal path

server-native detects Bun runtime and provides a fast-path fetch handler that performs lookupRoute() + handler dispatch without constructing a full TypoKitRequest for routes with no middleware, no validators, and bodyless methods (GET/HEAD/DELETE)
Fast-path handler extracts method and path from req.url using indexOf/substring — no new URL()
Fast-path handler calls the registered handler directly with a minimal request shape: { method, path, params, headers: req.headers, query, body: undefined }
Fast-path handler returns new Response(JSON.stringify(body), { status: 200, headers: jsonHeadersConst }) directly
Falls back to the full normalizeRequest→handleRequest→buildResponse path for routes with middleware, validators, or POST/PUT/PATCH bodies
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test server-native && pnpm nx test platform-bun
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck platform-bun && pnpm nx typecheck server-native
userStories.notes: Depends on US-021, US-022, US-023, US-028 (Bun platform optimizations must be in place first)

userStories.title: Bun — direct Bun.serve() handler without createBunServer wrapper
userStories.description: As a framework developer, I want server-native on Bun to call Bun.serve() directly inside its listen() method with an inline fetch handler instead of going through the createBunServer() abstraction. The current path is: server-native.listen() → import platform-bun → createBunServer(handleRequest) → Bun.serve(). Each layer adds function call overhead and async boundaries.
acceptanceCriteria: server-native on Bun calls Bun.serve() directly inside its listen() method, constructing the fetch handler inline using the globalThis.Bun reference
The inline fetch handler implements the fast-path from US-020 (direct route lookup → handler → Response) for simple routes and falls back to full normalize→handle→buildResponse for complex routes
Import only normalizeRequestSync, normalizeRequestAsync, and buildResponse from @typokit/platform-bun — do NOT import createBunServer
createBunServer() remains exported from platform-bun for standalone use but is no longer used by server-native internally
All existing tests pass: pnpm nx test server-native && pnpm nx test platform-bun
Typecheck passes: pnpm nx typecheck server-native
userStories.notes: Depends on US-020 (Bun fast-path). This is the final integration story that ties all Bun optimizations together.
